title: CrispyTivi Source Registry

provider_types.summary: Direct playlist URL with optional guide pairing and catch-up support.

capabilities.title: Live TV
capabilities.summary: Map playlist rows to channels and categories.

capabilities.title: Guide
capabilities.summary: Pair XMLTV for EPG coverage and better browse context.

capabilities.title: Catch-up
capabilities.summary: Use archive URLs when the source exposes timeshift.

capabilities.title: Movies
capabilities.summary: Not a native catalog lane, but playable media can still surface.

capabilities.title: Series
capabilities.summary: Series metadata depends on the external playlist and guide pairing.

auth.summary: No account credentials are needed for a direct playlist URL.
helper_lines: Use this lane for remote playlist URLs.
Guide pairing is optional but recommended.

import.summary: Playlist can import once URLs validate.

provider_types.onboarding_hint: Start with a direct URL, then add XMLTV if available.

provider_types.summary: On-device playlist import from a local file or mounted storage path.

capabilities.title: Live TV
capabilities.summary: Load channels from a local playlist file.

capabilities.title: Guide
capabilities.summary: Pair XMLTV locally or from a nearby import source.

capabilities.title: Local file
capabilities.summary: Import from a file path or attached storage target.

capabilities.title: Catch-up
capabilities.summary: Not advertised unless the imported playlist carries archive data.

auth.summary: Local files do not require credentials.
helper_lines: Use this lane for attached storage or imported files.
File validation should happen before import starts.

import.summary: Local playlist import is complete and ready to browse.

provider_types.onboarding_hint: Choose a local playlist file, then pair guide data if needed.

provider_types.summary: Account-backed provider with live, movies, series, and guide lanes.

capabilities.title: Live TV
capabilities.summary: Portal groups can surface channel lists and live categories.

capabilities.title: Movies
capabilities.summary: Catalog data exposes a movie lane with browse and detail views.

capabilities.title: Series
capabilities.summary: Series catalogs can feed seasons, episodes, and resume state.

capabilities.title: Guide
capabilities.summary: EPG data can support channel schedule overlays and browse grids.

capabilities.title: Catch-up
capabilities.summary: Archive playback is available when the portal exposes it.

health.summary: Portal access is waiting for valid credentials.

auth.summary: Credentials are required before catalog sync can continue.
helper_lines: Use the portal endpoint supplied by the provider.
Validation should happen before import begins.

import.summary: Import is paused until auth succeeds.

provider_types.onboarding_hint: Authenticate first, then import catalog data and guide fields.

provider_types.summary: MAG/Stalker portal with device-backed authentication and live/media lanes.

capabilities.title: Live TV
capabilities.summary: Device-backed portals can surface channel lists and current-state browse.

capabilities.title: Movies
capabilities.summary: Portal catalogs expose movie browsing and detail lanes.

capabilities.title: Series
capabilities.summary: Series catalogs can populate seasons, episodes, and resume behavior.

capabilities.title: Guide
capabilities.summary: Portal EPG data can drive channel schedules and overlays.

capabilities.title: Catch-up
capabilities.summary: Archive playback depends on portal support and device session state.

health.summary: Portal session requires reconnect to refresh the device token.

auth.summary: The portal session expired and needs a device reconnect.
helper_lines: Keep portal credentials separate from playlist flows.
Device-backed sessions must unwind safely if validation fails.

import.summary: Catalog refresh is running after reconnect.

provider_types.onboarding_hint: Reconnect the device, then let the portal refresh its catalog and guide state.

steps.title: Choose source type
steps.summary: Select the provider family first so later fields match the right import and auth model.
helper_lines: Keep source/provider selection inside Settings-owned flows.
Wizard steps stay ordered and reversible.

steps.title: Add connection details
steps.summary: Capture the endpoint and source-specific path before auth or validation runs.
helper_lines: Connection validation should fail here instead of later import screens.
Temporary connection state must not auto-restore into a stale step.

steps.title: Authenticate source
steps.summary: Sensitive credentials stay in the wizard and should never auto-restore into a secret-bearing step.
helper_lines: Auth can be entered for new sources or reconnect flows on existing sources.
Back from this step returns safely to connection rather than leaving the user in a broken state.

steps.title: Choose import scope
steps.summary: Review what the source will bring in and confirm the validation result before final import begins.
helper_lines: Import confirmation is a dedicated step, not a hidden side effect of auth.
Failures here should unwind cleanly back through the wizard.

steps.title: Finish setup
steps.summary: Complete the source handoff and return to source overview with health and capability status visible.
helper_lines: Success returns to the Settings-owned source overview, not to a detached source domain.
The next runtime phases can rely on this onboarding lane being complete.

provider_copy.title: Remote playlist
provider_copy.summary: Use a direct M3U URL when the provider exposes a playlist endpoint.
helper_lines: Best for direct playlist URLs and optional guide pairing.
No account credentials are required unless the playlist is gated.

provider_copy.title: Local file
provider_copy.summary: Use a local file when the playlist is stored on device or mounted storage.
helper_lines: Good for offline or imported playlist workflows.
Guide data can be paired after the file is validated.

provider_copy.title: Account-backed portal
provider_copy.summary: Use Xtream when the provider exposes a username/password portal model.
helper_lines: Credentials are required before catalog import can begin.
Live TV, movies, series, and EPG can all be populated from the portal.

provider_copy.title: Device-backed portal
provider_copy.summary: Use Stalker when the provider expects a MAG-style device portal connection.
helper_lines: Portal URL plus device identifiers are required.
Reconnect and refresh should happen safely inside the wizard.

registry_notes: Provider metadata stays Rust-owned so Flutter can consume a stable snapshot later.
The registry models both capability support and current state for each provider family.
Auth and import progress are explicit so the onboarding wizard can render status without guessing.
